content_of_post_to_comment: I (18f) went through a very weird (un)schooling situation for the majority of my life. I have passed all of my GED tests with the exception of math, as I basically have to reteach myself starting from basic arithmetic all the way up to precalculus. It'll take 6-11 months depending on how much time I dedicate to studying every day. I was supposed to start doing this months ago. I was supposed to have taken all of my GED tests by now... there are sixish community college prerequisite classes that I should have signed up for by now (need my GED to take these) so that I could apply for the healthcare degree I want to do. Deadline for that application is August 1st of next year. I have to keep postponing all of these plans I have for myself since I cannot get anything done. As soon as I sit down and open my laptop to study the anxiety just pools in my stomach. My heart will begin to race. I don't know why I feel this way considering the alternative is that I end up without an education/job. Why does bettering my life feel vomit inducing? I've had multiple panic attacks just this month and it only leads to me crying on the floor for hours because it's like my body is physically holding me back from getting things done.
